description: Canonical agent-hook policy for oak-mcp-ecosystem. Guardrail-only: grounding reminder, quality-gate reminder, and blocking dangerous git/history-bypass commands.

preToolUse.description: Block shell commands that bypass safety guardrails, destroy history, or overwrite live working-tree edits. Working-tree-overwrite commands (checkout/restore variants) are blocked because they silently throw away in-flight edits — including peer-agent edits in parallel sessions — and `git fsck --lost-found` cannot recover them. Wildcard staging commands (`git add -A` / `--all` / `.`) are blocked under the explicit-pathspec discipline (distilled.md §Stage by explicit pathspec) so a continuity-deferral commit cannot accidentally bundle parallel-thread plan work or unrelated plugin enables. We never use git to remove work; forward-going filesystem changes only. See `.agent/rules/never-use-git-to-remove-work.md`.

preToolUseContent.description: Block agents from writing user-approved exception markers into file content. Only the project owner can approve eslint-disable exceptions with the JC: prefix.

sessionStart.practice_box_check: Check .agent/practice-core/incoming/ for new Practice Core files.
sessionStart.description: Remind the agent to ground itself at session start.

preCommit.quality_gate_reminder: Run the required quality gates; `pnpm check` is the canonical aggregate gate.
preCommit.description: Remind the agent to run quality gates before committing.

claude_code.notes: Bash PreToolUse is natively activated for blocked-pattern enforcement. sessionStart and preCommit remain documented-only because grounding and quality-gate reminders already live in rules, skills, and onboarding flows.

cursor.notes: No native agent hook surface at time of writing.
